originalBehavior: The published article loads through a route-local HTML shell plus shared CSS, a page script, Pixi runtime, and Plotly bundle, then initializes many simulation mounts and static charts across one longform page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published article structure, route-local CSS, Pixi runtime, Plotly bundle, and page script while rewriting only the subpath-hosting metadata, asset paths, and shared-footer provenance hook.
notes: The public body no longer exposes the upstream logo link, newsletter footer, social links, or other author-promo surfaces.
The route keeps the original article structure instead of condensing the simulations into a custom rewrite.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the published article shell, local script loading, simulation mounts, and final playground controls as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route loads locally, the simulations mount, the graphs render, and the public route stays responsive without upstream runtime dependencies.

originalBehavior: The published article opens with a progression of load-balancer simulations that demonstrate round robin, queues, server variance, weighted variants, and least-connections behavior.
localStatus: The local route keeps the opening simulation progression intact under the original published Pixi runtime.
notes: The route preserves the automatically starting and visibility-aware simulation behavior rather than replacing the scenes with static screenshots.
The earliest simulation mounts remain in the same article positions as the published page.
evidence: The route-specific smoke contract includes one early simulation mount and one drag-free interaction path through the final control panel.
Shared smoke coverage verifies an early simulation canvas initializes before the article graphs and final playground.

originalBehavior: The published article renders several Plotly-based latency and drop graphs, then closes with a full playground that dynamically injects algorithm and slider controls for a large final simulation.
localStatus: The local route keeps the Plotly graphs and the final playground intact, with the original script still creating the algorithm selector and slider controls at runtime.
notes: The final playground still exposes the algorithm selector, RPS slider, variance sliders, and server-count slider under the original runtime.
The route preserves the published medians, percentile, histogram, and dropped-request chart sections instead of replacing them with a static summary.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one chart mount and one final playground control path as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies a Plotly graph mounts and the final playground controls appear and respond to input.

originalBehavior: The published article closes with discussion links and an upstream footer that includes newsletter, social, and author provenance surfaces.
localStatus: The local route keeps the article close intact while leaving the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface and pruning the self-promotional social link from the body discussion note.
notes: Third-party discussion links and technical references remain intact, but author-promo and newsletter surfaces are removed.
The route does not require upstream asset URLs or author-promo widgets at runtime.
evidence: The public-surface audit is extended for the Sam Rose family so creator, share, and newsletter links are caught outside the shared footer and docs.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route remains runtime-clean and responsive after the provenance cleanup.
